Package org.eventb.internal.core.ast.wd

Class Node

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.ast.wd.Node
Direct Known Subclasses:
NodeForAll, NodeLand, NodeLimp, NodePred
public abstract class Node extends Object
An abstract base class for all nodes of the tree that would be used to simplify the WD Lemma.

The tree will be created when the method improve()from WDImprover will be called.There are four types of nodes

  • NODE_FORALL
  • NODE_IMPLICATION
  • NODE_CONJONCTION
  • NODE_PREDICATE

    • Example: The following formula 

      ∀ x, y · 
      x ↦ y∈p_at 
        ⇒       
             y∈dom(p_c_inv)
           ∧ p_c_inv∈ CO ⇸ CO
           ∧ x∈dom(p_c_inv) 
           ∧ p_c_inv∈ CO ⇸ CO
    will be represented as 
     FORALL
  LIMP
   x ↦ y ∈p_at
    LAND
     y ∈ dom(p_c_inv)
     p_c_inv∈CO ⇸ CO
     x ∈ dom(p_c_inv)
     p_c_inv∈CO ⇸ CO

    In this class, when a method appears in two versions : x and internalx, internalx is the method intended to be overrided in other classes and x is the method that would be called externally.

    • Field Details

      • subsumed

        private boolean subsumed
        if true, the tree node could be substituted by the a node with true predicate because the corresponding sub-predicate is subsumed by another one. If false, it means that the constructed tree will be identical to the original one.

    • Constructor Details

      • Node

        public Node()

    • Method Details

      • simplifyTree

        public Predicate simplifyTree(FormulaBuilder fb)
        Simplifies the tree representing a formula by following these different steps :

      • Finds the maximal number of universal quantifiers between the root of the formula and any leaf node.
      • Uses this maximal binding depth to applies an offset to all the bound identifiers to allow the comparison between all the sub-predicates.
      • Finds and simplifies subsumed implications using a fresh empty set to collect the antecedents
      • Uses the simplified tree to build a new AST.

        • Parameters:
        fb - formula builder to use
        Returns:
        Predicate The simplified AST

      • maxBindingDepth

        protected abstract int maxBindingDepth()
        Returns the depth of a tree (maximal number of quantifiers associated with a predicate).

        Example : "x∈dom(f)∧f∈ℤ ⇸ ℤ∧y∈dom(f)". The returned depth will be 0.

        Example : "∀f·f∈S ⇸ S ⇒(∀x·x∈dom(f) ∧(f(x)=f(x) ⇒ (∀y·y∈dom(f))))". The returned depth will be 3.

        Returns:
        depth of the tree

      • boundIdentifiersEqualizer

        protected abstract void boundIdentifiersEqualizer(int offset)
        Applies an offset to the bound identifiers from an amount depending on the deepness of this predicate in the tree to allow comparison between predicates with different depth.

        The offset is equal to 0 for predicates having the maximal number of universal quantifiers associated to them, and to the maximal binding depth for predicates with no quantifiers associated. This transformation is equivalent to move all the universal quantifiers to the root of the formula.

        Example : "∀ x·x∈dom(f)⇒(∀y·x∈dom(f)∧f∈S⇸S∧y∈dom(f)∧f∈S⇸S)". The two "x∈dom(f)" predicates have different bound identifier indexes in this WD Lemma and we need to apply an offset of 1 to the first predicate if we want an exact comparison between predicates. The bound identifiers indexes will be equals to the indexes of ∀ x,y·x∈dom(f)⇒(x∈dom(f)∧f∈S⇸S∧y∈dom(f)∧f∈S⇸S).

        Parameters:
        offset - offset applied to the bound identifiers

      • asPredicate

        protected final Predicate asPredicate(FormulaBuilder fb, boolean original)
        Builds a new AST using the simplified tree
        Parameters:
        fb - formula builder to use for building the result
        original - true to get the original predicate, false to get the predicate with normalized bound identifiers
        Returns:
        Predicate The simplified AST

      • internalAsPredicate

        protected abstract Predicate internalAsPredicate(FormulaBuilder fb, boolean original)

      • collectAntecedents

        protected abstract void collectAntecedents(Set<Predicate> antecedents, FormulaBuilder fb)
        Collects the antecedents for simplifying lemmas. This method should be called with a set with the previous known antecedents, on the left-hand side of implication nodes to allow comparison between each side of the implication.
        Parameters:
        antecedents - set of antecedents to complete
        fb - formula builder to use

      • addPredicateToSet

        protected final void addPredicateToSet(Set<Predicate> set, FormulaBuilder fb)
        Adds the predicate for this node to the given set, if it isn't subsumed. Conversely, if this predicate was already present in the given set, this predicate becomes subsumed.
        Parameters:
        set - set where predicates will be added
        fb - formula builder to use

      • simplify

        protected final void simplify(Set<Lemma> knownLemmas, Set<Predicate> antecedents, FormulaBuilder fb)
        Traverses the formula, marking as subsumed leaf nodes that are subsumed.
        Parameters:
        knownLemmas - lemmas that are currently known to hold
        antecedents - left-hand side context of this node to be used for building new lemmas
        fb - formula builder to use

      • internalSimplify

        protected abstract void internalSimplify(Set<Lemma> knownLemmas, Set<Predicate> antecedents, FormulaBuilder fb)

      • toString

        public final String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class Object

      • toString

        protected final void toString(StringBuilder sb, String indent)
        Creates a string representation of the tree, avoiding to represent subsumed sub-tree.
        Parameters:
        sb - stringBuilder to use
        indent - string that will be added to the stringBuilder

      • internalToString

        protected abstract void internalToString(StringBuilder sb, String indent)

      • setNodeSubsumed

        protected final void setNodeSubsumed()
        Indicates that the predicate corresponding to this node is subsumed by another predicate.
